title: 'Alien: Romulus' leads worldwide box office in static late-summer session; 'Afraid' disappoints
text: The global box office remained stagnant during the late summer period, with the top four films maintaining their previous positions. "Alien: Romulus" dominated for its third consecutive week, earning an estimated $31.9 million and bringing its worldwide total to $283.4 million. This represents the lowest chart-topping total since late April, when the summer blockbuster season began. The lack of significant new releases contributed to the subdued market activity. Disney maintained its six-week hold on the global box office chart, first through Marvel's "Deadpool & Wolverine" and subsequently with 20th Century Studios' "Alien: Romulus". The sci-fi thriller ranks second in franchise history (unadjusted for inflation), surpassing 2017's "Alien: Covenant" ($240.9 million) and becoming the year's leading horror film. "Inside Out 2" reentered the top five, moving from sixth place. The Pixar animated film achieved $1.666 billion worldwide, surpassing "The Lion King" to rank ninth all-time. It requires only minimal additional revenue to overtake "Jurassic World". Sony's AI thriller "Afraid" disappointed, accumulating just $6.0 million from its opening weekend across North American and select international markets. The film placed ninth domestically with $3.7 million for the weekend.
